marketState: Neutral overall, tilting bullish short-term on strong cooling demand
summary: Front-month NG is at $2.922, essentially at model fair value ($2.917, spread +$0.005) and about 10.8pp below its seasonal median for this time of year (-14.2% YTD vs -3.4% median). Near-term (1-7D) and medium-term (8-16D) weather demand are running well above the 5-year normal (+22.7% and +10.1%, with the 1-7D reading near the top of its range at 0.91 and still rising, +11.8 24h), a bullish input the market doesn't look to have fully priced given price sits right on the fair-value line. Working against that: storage is +184 Bcf above the 5-year average with a +72 Bcf bearish build surprise, managed money is net short (-60,295 contracts, ratio 0.8, though only 35th percentile of the 5y range), and daily/weekly technicals show a bearish trend structure (50<200 EMA, RSI 35.9/37.5). Seasonality itself is only weakly bearish (38/38/50/31% chance of being higher over 1-4 weeks, typical 4-week move -3.1%) but reliability is explicitly 'Low' (50pp spread), so it shouldn't be weighted heavily. Net: fundamentals are genuinely split between bullish near-term weather and bearish storage/positioning/trend, favoring a tactical weather-driven long over 1-10 days while staying sidelined on the multi-week trade until storage or seasonality data firm up.

longTerm.rationale: Longer-term signals conflict: above-normal weather demand and a rising fair-value curve ($2.917 now to $3.049 by +21D) argue for upside, while a +184 Bcf storage surplus with a bearish surprise, a net-short/bearish-tilted COT, and bearish daily/weekly technicals (50<200 EMA, RSI <40) argue for downside. Low-reliability seasonality (50pp spread) shouldn't tip the balance either way.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for one of: (a) a bullish storage print or shrinking 5y surplus confirming tightening, (b) a pullback into the $2.55-$2.70 value zone while 1-7D weather demand stays >15% above 5y normal, for a lower-risk long, or (c) daily close/RSI confirming the bearish EMA structure resolving lower toward $2.60 for a short.

shortTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; trim half at TP1, trail remainder to TP2
shortTerm.rationale: 1-7D weather demand is +22.7% above 5-year normal near the top of its range (0.91) and still rising, yet front price sits exactly at fair value ($2.922 vs $2.917 fair, +$0.005 spread) with a modest front-over-next backwardation ($2.922 vs $2.888) - hot near-term demand isn't showing up as a premium yet. Intraday technicals are constructive (15m trend bullish, RSI 45-53 neutral, 5m/15m EMA 50>200), supporting a tactical long into the demand spike.
shortTerm.invalidation: A close below $2.80, or 1-7D weather demand fading back toward/below the 5y normal, invalidates this trade.

keyDrivers: Near-term weather demand +22.7% vs 5y, rangePos 0.91 and rising - strongest bullish input
Storage +184 Bcf above 5y average with a +72 Bcf bearish build surprise - key bearish overhang
Price sits right at fair value ($2.922 vs $2.917) despite above-normal demand
Managed money net short (-60,295, ratio 0.8) but only 35th percentile - not an extreme signal
Daily/weekly technicals bearish (50<200 EMA, RSI <40) vs neutral-to-bullish intraday (15m)
risks: Seasonality reliability is explicitly Low (50pp spread) - don't over-weight the -3.1% typical 4wk move
Futures curve object is null in the snapshot - spread read is limited to the front/next price gap only
Storage surprises and weather forecasts can flip quickly in cooling season, invalidating either trade fast
disclaimer: Informational only, not financial advice. Auto-generated hourly.
